senses.definition: used to tell someone to change their life style and start doing more exciting or important things

examples.x: You guys should just stop moaning and get a life!
Instead of constantly watching TV, you should get a life and explore some hobbies or activities.
She told her friend to get a life and stop obsessing over her ex-boyfriend's social media posts.
Instead of gossiping about others, it's better to get a life and focus on personal growth.

word_list.pronunciation: pˌʊt stˈʌf stˈɪk ɐ sˈɑːk ɪn ɪt

senses.definition: to tell a person to stop making noise or stop talking, especially if they are being loud or annoying

examples.x: Hey, put a sock in it, will you?
I was just about to tell them about Janet's pregnancy, but I stuffed a sock in it when she shot me an icy look.
I wish someone would tell him to put a sock in it.
I love family gatherings, but sometimes Uncle Joe needs a reminder to put a sock in it during the speeches.
During the tense meeting, the manager had to tell the team to stick a sock in it and focus on finding solutions to the problem.
The loud music from the neighboring apartment was disturbing everyone, so I finally went over and asked them to stuff a sock in it.

senses.definition: used to tell someone to leave immediately

examples.x: Beat it, little brother—I've got things to do.
There's nothing for you to see here; just beat it and let us work.
The manager told the unwanted solicitor to beat it and not bother the employees.
The teacher asked the disruptive student to beat it and not disrupt the class.
When the party was over, they told the remaining guests to beat it and go home.

senses.definition: used to wish a person good luck, particularly before their performance

examples.x: Jason sent Phillip a fax from the airport before Monday's show, with the greeting: `Break a leg and enjoy yourself.'
Before the play, the director said to the cast, 'Break a leg,' to wish them success.
She whispered to her friend backstage, "Break a leg out there; you'll do great!"
The band members gave each other a pep talk and said, 'Break a leg' before going on stage.

senses.definition: used to tell someone that they should stop saying or doing a particular thing

examples.x: Come off it now – she was only trying to help.
Come off it, you can't expect us to believe that you caught a fish the size of a car.
She said she won the race by a mile, and her friends replied, "Come off it; it was a close finish."
He told us he could make us millionaires overnight; we had to tell him to come off it.

senses.definition: used to tell a whining person that one does not care

examples.x: Cry me a river, I don't even have a cleaning lady.
She's been complaining about that minor inconvenience all day; someone should tell her to cry me a river.
When he grumbled about his workload, his coworker jokingly said, "Cry me a river; we're all busy."
He's been whining about the weather for hours, and I finally told him to cry me a river.
She was upset about losing a game, and her friend said, "It's just a game; cry me a river."

senses.definition: used to tell a person to stop talking about unimportant or incorrect things

examples.x: Cut the crap and tell me what really happened.
I don't have time for your excuses; just cut the crap and tell me the truth.
When the salesman kept giving vague answers, the customer said, "Cut the shit and give me the facts.
Quit trying to impress us with your stories; it's time to cut the crap and get to the point.
She knew he was avoiding the issue, so she firmly told him, "Cut the shit, and let's talk honestly."

senses.definition: used to tell a person to stop doing something or talking about a certain subject that is annoying one

examples.x: Give it a rest, will you?
She's been complaining about her job all day; someone needs to tell her to give it a rest.
He kept asking the same question repeatedly, so I finally told him to give it a rest.

senses.definition: used to show one's disbelief or disagreement about what someone has just said

examples.x: Give me a break!

senses.definition: used to show one's indifference toward one's actions or mood

examples.x: If you really think it's your prerogative to tell me how to run my business, then you can go hang yourself for all I care.
Why don't you go hang yourself instead?
He kept bothering me with irrelevant questions, so I finally told him to go hang himself.

senses.definition: used to tell a person to go and leave one alone
used humorously when one is about to attempt to do something foolish or dangerous, often to showoff or outdo someone

examples.x: Hold my beer; I'm going to challenge the reigning champion to an arm-wrestling contest.

senses.definition: used to show one's disregard or lack of respect for someone or something

examples.x: Kiss my ass, Jerry!
Some guy tried hitting on me at the bar, so I told him to kiss my ass, and I walked off!

senses.definition: used to express one's desire to stop someone from doing something
used to tell someone to stop talking

examples.x: Hey, none of your lip!
None of your lip, young man; you're still grounded for not following the rules.
I told her, 'None of your lip; you're serving detention for disrupting the class.
The coach said, 'None of your lip; you're on the bench for the first half of the game.'
The manager sternly replied, 'None of your lip; your behavior at work needs improvement.

senses.definition: used to ask a person to pay attention to one's words and believe them

examples.x: Read my lips, there will be no changes to the company's vacation policy this year," the CEO announced.
I'm telling you, I will not accept any more excuses—read my lips, we need this project completed by Friday.
"Read my lips, there will be no changes to the company's vacation policy this year," the CEO announced.
She leaned in and whispered, "Read my lips, the surprise party is on Saturday, and it's a secret."
The teacher declared, "Read my lips, there will be no exceptions to the homework deadline."

word_list.word: say it, do not spray it

senses.definition: used to tell someone to not spit when they are talking
used to tell someone to ignore or disregard something that was just said

examples.x: I initially planned to go to the beach this weekend, but scratch that; I have to work overtime.
I was going to order pizza for dinner, but scratch that, I'll cook something at home.
I thought we would need more chairs for the party, but scratch that, some guests can stand.
I was considering buying a new phone, but scratch that, I'll stick with my current one.
I had scheduled a meeting for Friday morning, but scratch that; I'll reschedule it for next week.

senses.definition: used when urging a person to quickly say the thing they seem embarrassed or frightened to share

examples.x: I can see you have something on your mind; just spit it out, and we'll talk about it.
You've been dancing around the topic all evening; why don't you just spit it out and tell us what's bothering you?
I know you're keeping something from me; it's time to spit it out and let me know what's going on.
You don't need to be so secretive; just spit it out, and we can figure this out together.
I can tell there's more to the story; spit it out and don't keep me in the dark.

senses.definition: used to tell someone to stop bothering one and leave one alone

examples.x: I've had enough of your complaints; why don't you take a hike and give us some peace?
If you can't be helpful, then take a hike and let us finish the project without your interference.
He's been bothering us all day; it's time for him to take a hike and leave us alone.
You're not welcome at this party, so I suggest you take a hike before things get unpleasant
I can't deal with your excuses right now; please take a hike and come back when you're ready to work.

senses.definition: to go away and leave a person alone

examples.x: Listen, I don't want to buy any, so why don't you just take a walk and leave me alone!
If you need some time to think, why don't you take a walk and come back when you're ready?
I'm not interested in your excuses, so you can take a walk.

senses.definition: used to tell someone to take care of themselves when saying goodbye to each other
